title: Narrative Arc — Clarity as an Economic Instrument
recognition: Organizations often treat clarity as a soft communication preference rather than an operating capability.
reframe: Ambiguity consumes time, trust, and coordination energy, making clarity an economic instrument.
framework: Priorities, ownership, constraints, decision rights, escalation paths, and operating principles are the architecture of clarity.
proof: The draft grounds the argument in recurring forms of translation, escalation, rework, and decision latency while explicitly withholding unverified quantitative claims.
application: Executives can design clarity into the system instead of relying on speeches, memos, or personality.
consequence: A clearer system spends less energy reconstructing intent and more energy executing against it.
invitation: Evaluate clarity as an operating property that can be designed, measured, and improved.
